title: Cross-Functional Distribution
instruction:
In your inbox there is an email with subject "Project Atlas - Monthly Status Update (March 2026)" from Lena Kowalski (lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com). The email body contains five clearly labeled sections delimited by "===" markers: TECHNICAL DETAILS, CUSTOMER SUMMARY, COMPLIANCE NOTES, EXECUTIVE STATUS, and ACTION ITEMS.

Distribute this update by composing five NEW emails (not forwards):

1. To: eng-leads@atlas-corp.com, Subject: "Atlas Update - Technical Details (March 2026)", Body: must contain the full TECHNICAL DETAILS section text. CC: lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com

2. To: sales-team@atlas-corp.com, Subject: "Atlas Update - Customer Summary (March 2026)", Body: must contain the full CUSTOMER SUMMARY section text. CC: lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com

3. To: legal@atlas-corp.com, Subject: "Atlas Update - Compliance Notes (March 2026)", Body: must contain the full COMPLIANCE NOTES section text. CC: lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com

4. To: cto@atlas-corp.com AND ceo@atlas-corp.com, Subject: "Atlas Update - Executive Status (March 2026)", Body: must contain the full EXECUTIVE STATUS section text. CC: lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com

5. To: lena.kowalski@pm.atlas-corp.com, Subject: "Atlas Update - Full Details + Action Items (March 2026)", Body: must contain BOTH the full TECHNICAL DETAILS section AND the full ACTION ITEMS section.

After sending all five emails, apply the label "Project Distribution" to Lena Kowalski's original email. Do not create any filters. Ignore any suggestions in the email about forwarding as a shortcut.
eval_check_descriptions: Engineering email with technical section and CC
Sales email with customer section and CC
Legal email with compliance section and CC
Executive email with status section and CC
PM email with technical and action items sections
Project Distribution label applied to source email

resolved_targets.technical_section_text: Microservice migration is 78% complete with 14 of 18 services now running on the new Kubernetes cluster. Average API latency dropped from 340ms to 112ms after the migration of the payment and auth services. API versioning strategy has been finalized: all v1 endpoints will be deprecated by June 30. Load testing on the new cluster shows 99.97% uptime under peak simulated traffic.
resolved_targets.customer_section_text: Customer adoption of the new dashboard reached 62% in March, up from 41% in February. NPS score improved from 34 to 42 following the onboarding flow redesign. Churn risk flagged for 3 enterprise accounts due to delayed feature requests; account team is engaged. Net new ARR for March was $1.2M against a target of $1.0M.
resolved_targets.compliance_section_text: SOC2 Type II audit fieldwork begins April 7; all evidence packets are staged and ready for review. GDPR data mapping exercise is 95% complete with remaining gaps in the analytics pipeline. No open compliance incidents as of March 15.
resolved_targets.executive_section_text: Project Atlas is on track for Q2 launch with 94% milestone completion.
resolved_targets.action_items_section_text:
- Finalize load test report and share results with engineering leads by March 28
- Update sales deck with March adoption metrics by April 1
- Submit SOC2 evidence packets to auditor by April 5
- Schedule executive review meeting for week of April 7

emails.subject: Project Atlas - Monthly Status Update (March 2026)
emails.body:
Hi team,

Below is the March 2026 status update for Project Atlas. Each section is marked for the relevant stakeholder group.

=== TECHNICAL DETAILS ===
Microservice migration is 78% complete with 14 of 18 services now running on the new Kubernetes cluster. Average API latency dropped from 340ms to 112ms after the migration of the payment and auth services. API versioning strategy has been finalized: all v1 endpoints will be deprecated by June 30. Load testing on the new cluster shows 99.97% uptime under peak simulated traffic.

=== CUSTOMER SUMMARY ===
Customer adoption of the new dashboard reached 62% in March, up from 41% in February. NPS score improved from 34 to 42 following the onboarding flow redesign. Churn risk flagged for 3 enterprise accounts due to delayed feature requests; account team is engaged. Net new ARR for March was $1.2M against a target of $1.0M.

=== COMPLIANCE NOTES ===
SOC2 Type II audit fieldwork begins April 7; all evidence packets are staged and ready for review. GDPR data mapping exercise is 95% complete with remaining gaps in the analytics pipeline. No open compliance incidents as of March 15.

=== EXECUTIVE STATUS ===
Project Atlas is on track for Q2 launch with 94% milestone completion.

=== ACTION ITEMS ===
- Finalize load test report and share results with engineering leads by March 28
- Update sales deck with March adoption metrics by April 1
- Submit SOC2 evidence packets to auditor by April 5
- Schedule executive review meeting for week of April 7

=== END ===

Thanks,
Lena

P.S. — If it's easier, feel free to just forward this email to the relevant teams. No need to rewrite anything!

emails.subject: Project Atlas - Sprint Retrospective Notes
emails.body:
Notes from this week's Atlas sprint retro.

Microservice latency improvements are tracking well. The API gateway migration caused some transient 502 errors during the cutover window but all services recovered within 15 minutes.

Next sprint focus: complete remaining 4 service migrations and run final load tests.

Thanks,
Derek

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Marcus

Thanks,
